1_dns_throttle.evidence: SkipThrottle now skips default+auth_login+auth_register. Root cause: bare @SkipThrottle() skips only 'default'; auth_login(5/60) applied to every route capping DNS at 5/min. Test dns-policy.throttle.spec rebuilt with the real 3-throttler prod config: 500 DNS calls never throttled; a normal route still throttles. Live PASS requires redeploy of 36c4e4d.
1_dns_throttle.risk: Public exposure: /dns/policy/check has no auth and is now unthrottled — restrict it to localhost/trusted network at Nginx (it is called only by the co-located DNS service).

2_not_executed.scheduler_gaming_expiry: PARTIAL (remaining-time detection tested; full checkActiveSessions cron path not driven)
2_not_executed.scheduler_cache_cleanup: NOT_IMPLEMENTED (no cache-cleanup cron exists in the codebase)
2_not_executed.ttl_cache_expiry: PARTIAL (30s TTL delegated to cache-manager; version-invalidation tested in dns-policy.engine; raw TTL expiry not asserted)

3_push.evidence: push.service.spec covers register/upsert, send, prune invalid token, no-tokens (offline device), delivery-disabled no-op. FcmSender unconfigured => 'skipped'.
3_push.gap: No retry logic implemented in PushService — not tested (would be a new feature; out of scope).

4_load.reason: 100/500/1000-device load needs a local/staging backend (Postgres+Redis). None available on this Windows host and Docker is disallowed; running it against production would DoS + pollute prod. Harness is built and gated behind SIM_ALLOW_HEAVY + non-prod URL.

5_chaos.client_side: PASS (high latency, 100% packet loss handled, unreachable backend handled)
5_chaos.server_side: NOT_EXECUTED (crash Redis/DB/backend, PM2 restart need host/SSH access; destructive on prod)

6_security.replay_attack: PASS (unit: auth.refresh-replay, real JWT + stateful store; fixed via unique jti + CAS)
6_security.expired_jwt: PARTIAL (invalid-signature -> 401 proven; a genuinely expired token not forged)
6_security.role_escalation: PASS (register role=ADMIN -> 400; PARENT hits admin route -> 403)
6_security.sql_injection: PASS (stored as literal, table intact, live)

7_regression.evidence: 53/53 backend tests green; no previously-passing scenario broken.

remainingRisks: Two critical fixes (unique-jti replay fix, DNS multi-throttler skip) are committed (36c4e4d) but NOT deployed — production is still vulnerable to replay + DNS 429 fail-open until redeploy.
/dns/policy/check is public + now unthrottled -> restrict to localhost at Nginx.

auth_login/auth_register throttlers are registered globally in forRoot and apply per-endpoint (5/60 & 10/60) to EVERY route, not just auth — a latent over-throttling of normal endpoints. Auth limiting itself is done via @Throttle on the auth controller, so these named throttlers are effectively spurious. Left unchanged (no-refactor directive) but flagged.
Load, server-side chaos, real-device, and console verification remain unrun (no local stack / hardware).
Push retry + cache-cleanup cron are not implemented.
